title: Week of March 23, 2026
summary: This week delivered major improvements to data reliability and system security across all platforms. We enhanced Salesforce data collection accuracy, expanded execution tracking to HubSpot and Marketo, and added comprehensive security scanning to prevent credential leaks. Additionally, we streamlined Windows installation and improved RevOps assessments with better revenue field detection.

entries.headline: More complete Salesforce automation discovery and tracking
details: OpsPal now captures all Salesforce workflow rules and flows without missing any automation components
Better tracking ensures your automation inventory reports are fully accurate

entries.headline: Execution tracking now works across HubSpot and Marketo
details: OpsPal can now track what actions were performed in HubSpot and Marketo, just like it does for Salesforce
This gives you better visibility into changes made across all your revenue systems

entries.headline: Safer operations on production Salesforce orgs
details: OpsPal now allows read-only data retrieval from production orgs without triggering safety warnings
You can investigate production issues more easily while maintaining strict protection against accidental changes

entries.headline: Automated release notes generation
details: OpsPal now automatically creates and publishes release notes from development activity
You get clearer, more regular updates on new features and improvements

entries.headline: Resolved connection issues with Salesforce operations
details: Fixed several cases where OpsPal was being overly cautious and blocking legitimate Salesforce queries
Operations that should work now complete successfully without unnecessary restrictions

entries.headline: Faster simple Salesforce record lookups
details: Single record queries and small result sets now run more efficiently
You get quicker responses when looking up individual accounts, contacts, or opportunities

entries.headline: Simplified Windows installation process
details: OpsPal now installs Claude Desktop, Node.js, and other required tools automatically on Windows
Windows users get the same smooth setup experience as Mac and Linux users

entries.headline: Better error detection and reporting
details: OpsPal now catches more types of connection and authentication errors early
You get clearer error messages when something goes wrong with your CRM connections

entries.headline: Windows installation script works with all PowerShell setups
details: The Windows installer now works correctly when run directly from the web
Fixes issues where the installation would fail on certain PowerShell configurations

entries.headline: RevOps assessments now include revenue context
details: Assessment results now capture which revenue fields your organization uses
This helps create more relevant recommendations based on your actual sales process

entries.headline: Customizable templates and branding
details: You can now customize OpsPal templates and branding elements while preserving changes through updates
Personalizations are stored safely and won't be overwritten when OpsPal updates

entries.headline: Automatic revenue field detection in Salesforce assessments
details: OpsPal now automatically finds your organization's revenue fields like ARR, ACV, and custom revenue metrics
RevOps assessments are more accurate because they analyze your actual revenue tracking setup

entries.headline: Deployment safety checks for Salesforce changes
details: New validation tools prevent problematic deployments like duplicate picklist values and field history overflows
Catches potential issues before they cause problems in your Salesforce org

entries.headline: Added credential leak protection
details: OpsPal now scans for accidentally committed passwords, tokens, and other sensitive information
Prevents security credentials from being exposed in your project files

entries.headline: Continuous security scanning for all projects
details: OpsPal now automatically scans all code changes for security vulnerabilities
Helps ensure your RevOps automation projects maintain security best practices

entries.headline: Removed embedded credentials from system files
details: Cleaned up system files that contained hardcoded credentials
Improves overall security posture and follows security best practices

entries.headline: Salesforce deployment validation works more reliably
details: Fixed issues where deployment checks would fail due to incorrect flow analysis
Deployment validations now complete successfully and catch real issues more accurately
